🌿 My Garden — Plant Catalogue

34 plants · Batches 1 & 2 · duplicates removed · some IDs to be confirmed

Batch 1

Moth Orchid

Phalaenopsis hybrid · Orchidaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Southeast Asia & Australia (tropical; modern plants are hybrids)
Care
Bright indirect light; no direct midday sun. Water weekly by soaking bark medium then draining fully — never leave in standing water. Moderate humidity. Feed with dilute orchid fertiliser during growth.
Propagation
Keiki (offshoots) from flower stems; division of mature clumps.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor only. Keep above 16°C; min ~12°C.
Sowing
Not grown from seed at home.
Flowering
Any time; most often winter–spring. Blooms last 2–3 months.
Time to maturity
Bought near-mature; reblooms on an annual cycle.
Batch 1

Care
Grown in LECA (clay pebbles) — maintain reservoir with dilute feed. Bright indirect light.

To be confirmed once ID confirmed.
Hardiness
Likely tender — indoor.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
To be confirmed.
Time to maturity

Batch 1

Closed Terrarium (mixed)

Mixed — ferns, moss, Fittonia, Soleirolia · Mixed
Ornamental
Origin
Tropical understorey species (varied)
Care
Self-contained humid ecosystem. Bright indirect light only. Rarely needs watering once balanced; light mist if condensation disappears. Open occasionally to vent.
Propagation
Division of moss/ferns; stem cuttings of Fittonia.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor only.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Grown for foliage.
Time to maturity
Maintained by trimming.
Photo unclear — clearer image would help identify each species precisely.
Batch 1

Spider Plant (glass terrarium vase)

Chlorophytum comosum 'Variegatum' · Asparagaceae
OrnamentalAir-purifying
Origin
Southern Africa
Care
Bright to moderate indirect light. Water when top few cm dry. Brown tips usually mean fluoride/chlorine in tap water — use filtered or left-to-stand water.
Propagation
Plantlets ('spiderettes') on runners root easily in water or compost; division.
Hardiness
Tender indoors; can summer outside, bring in below ~10°C.
Sowing
N/A — propagated by plantlets.
Flowering
Small white flowers on runners, mainly summer.
Time to maturity
Fast; produces runners within 1–2 years.
Batch 1

Lemon Tree

Citrus × limon · Rutaceae
EdibleOrnamental
Origin
South & Southeast Asia (cultivated worldwide)
Care
Full sun, sheltered position. Stand outside late spring–summer; overwinter frost-free in a cool bright room or greenhouse. Use citrus-specific feed. Consistent moisture, never waterlogged; citrus compost.
Propagation
Semi-ripe cuttings in summer; grafting (commercial); seed (slow and variable).
Hardiness
Half-hardy/tender — protect below ~5°C; not frost hardy.
Sowing
Cuttings taken mid-summer.
Flowering
Fragrant white blossom spring–summer; fruit takes several months to ripen.
Time to maturity
3–5+ years to reliable cropping.
Batch 1

Calla / Arum Lily

Zantedeschia aethiopica · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Southern Africa
Care
Moist rich soil; sun or part shade; tolerates boggy conditions. Keep well watered during growth. Mulch crown in winter if outdoors.
Propagation
Division of rhizomes in spring; seed (slow).
Hardiness
Borderline hardy in UK (to ~-5°C with mulch); coloured hybrids are tender.
Sowing
Plant/divide rhizomes in spring.
Flowering
White spathes early–mid summer.
Time to maturity
Flowers in 1–2 seasons from division.
All parts toxic if eaten; sap irritates skin.
Batch 1

Canna Lily (dark-leaved)

Canna × generalis · Cannaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Central & South America
Care
Full sun, rich moist soil. Generous feeding and watering in summer. Lift rhizomes after first frost or mulch heavily; store dry and frost-free over winter.
Propagation
Division of rhizomes in spring; seed (scarify hard coat first).
Hardiness
Tender — rhizomes killed by hard frost; lift or protect over winter.
Sowing
Start rhizomes indoors Feb–Apr; plant out after last frost.
Flowering
Orange/yellow flowers mid-summer to autumn.
Time to maturity
Flowers in first season from started rhizomes.
Batch 1

Fiddle-leaf Fig

Ficus lyrata · Moraceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Western Africa (lowland rainforest)
Care
Bright indirect light; rotate for even growth. Water when top 3–5 cm dry. Dislikes sudden moves, draughts and overwatering. Wipe leaves; feed in growing season.
Propagation
Stem/leaf-bud cuttings; air-layering.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor only, keep above ~15°C.
Sowing
N/A indoors.
Flowering
Does not flower indoors.
Time to maturity
Slow–moderate; tall specimen takes several years.
Milky sap is an irritant; mildly toxic to pets.

Care
Tall, dark glossy leaves; braided trunk. Care to be confirmed once ID is verified.

Likely tender — indoor.

N/A
Flowering

Batch 1

Peperomia

Peperomia sp. · Piperaceae
OrnamentalPet-safe
Origin
Tropical & subtropical regions worldwide; most species from South America
Care
Bright indirect light. Thick leaves store water — allow to dry between waterings; very susceptible to root rot if overwatered. No humidity required.
Propagation
Leaf or stem cuttings root very easily; division.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor only, above ~12°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Insignificant rat-tail flower spikes.
Time to maturity
Slow-moderate; compact grower.
Non-toxic / pet-safe. Exact species to be confirmed.
Batch 1

Alocasia 'Black Velvet'

Alocasia reginula · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Borneo (Southeast Asia)
Care
Bright indirect light, high humidity, warmth. Keep lightly moist but never soggy — rot-prone. Goes dormant in cold; reduce water in winter. Dislikes draughts.
Propagation
Division of offsets/corms at repotting.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor only, above ~16°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Rare indoors; grown for velvety foliage.
Time to maturity
Slow; stays compact ~30–40 cm.
Contains calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets if chewed.
Batch 1

Dracaena (variety to confirm)

Dracaena sp. · Asparagaceae
OrnamentalAir-purifying
Origin
Africa & Canary Islands (most species)
Care
Moderate to bright indirect light. Allow top of compost to dry between waterings. Sensitive to fluoride — use filtered water. Feed sparingly in growing season.
Propagation
Stem tip or cane cuttings.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~12°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Rare indoors.
Time to maturity
Slow to moderate.
⚠ Variety to be confirmed. Toxic to cats and dogs.
Batch 1

Corn Plant

Dracaena fragrans · Asparagaceae
OrnamentalAir-purifying
Origin
Tropical Africa
Care
Moderate to bright indirect light. Let top of medium dry; sensitive to fluoride (brown tips) — use filtered water. Grown in LECA here — maintain reservoir with dilute feed.
Propagation
Stem tip or cane section cuttings root readily.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~12°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Rare indoors (fragrant if it occurs).
Time to maturity
Slow; a tall cane plant takes years.
Toxic to cats and dogs.
Batch 1

Madagascar Dragon Tree

Dracaena marginata · Asparagaceae
OrnamentalAir-purifying
Origin
Madagascar
Care
Very tolerant. Bright indirect light best but survives lower. Drought tolerant — water sparingly and let dry out between waterings. Filtered water avoids tip burn.
Propagation
Tip and stem-section cuttings; very easy.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~12°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Very rare indoors.
Time to maturity
Slow; long-lived, eventually tall and architectural.
Toxic to cats and dogs.
Batch 1

Money Tree

Pachira aquatica · Malvaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Central & South America (wetlands)
Care
Bright indirect light. Water when top few cm dry; allow full drainage. Often sold with braided trunk. Fairly forgiving and adaptable.
Propagation
Seed; stem cuttings.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~12°C.
Sowing
Seed sown fresh in warmth.
Flowering
Does not flower indoors.
Time to maturity
Moderate; quickly makes a leafy small tree.
Batch 1

Velvet Calathea

Goeppertia rufibarba (syn. Calathea rufibarba) · Marantaceae
OrnamentalPet-safe
Origin
Brazil
Care
Indirect light only. Loves humidity; brown crispy edges mean dry air or hard/cold water. Keep lightly moist with filtered or rainwater, warm and draught-free. Moves leaves day to night (nyctinasty).
Propagation
Division at repotting in spring.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~16°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Insignificant indoors; grown for foliage.
Time to maturity
Moderate; clumps up over a couple of years.
Non-toxic / pet-safe.
Batch 1

Peace Lily

Spathiphyllum wallisii · Araceae
OrnamentalAir-purifyingToxic
Origin
Tropical Americas (Colombia, Venezuela)
Care
Moderate to low indirect light. Droops dramatically when thirsty — water and it recovers quickly. Use filtered water. Likes humidity. Feed in growing season.
Propagation
Division of clump at repotting.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~12°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
White spathes mainly spring–summer, often again in autumn.
Time to maturity
Fast; flowers readily once established.
Calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets if chewed.
Batch 2

Aloe (spotted)

Aloe aristata / hybrid · Asphodelaceae
OrnamentalMedicinal
Origin
South Africa
Care
Bright light, some direct sun. Very drought tolerant — water sparingly, allow to dry fully between waterings. Well-draining gritty compost. Avoid water in the rosette crown.
Propagation
Offsets (pups) detached and potted; seed.
Hardiness
Aloe aristata is one of the hardier aloes (survives light frost briefly); best kept frost-free in the UK.
Sowing
Sow seed at 21°C in spring; easier to propagate by offsets.
Flowering
Tall orange-red flower spikes in summer (visible in your photo — currently in flower!).
Time to maturity
Slow; offsets reach display size in 2–3 years.
Gel from leaves has traditional skin-soothing uses. Latex layer beneath skin can be irritating internally.
Batch 2

Echeveria

Echeveria sp. · Crassulaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Mexico & Central America
Care
Bright light with some direct sun for compact rosette shape. Very drought tolerant — water sparingly, let dry completely. Gritty well-draining compost. Avoid water sitting in rosette.
Propagation
Leaf cuttings (lay on dry compost); offsets; stem cuttings.
Hardiness
Tender — frost-free indoors or greenhouse over winter.
Sowing
Seed at 18–21°C in spring; leaf propagation much easier.
Flowering
Bell-shaped flowers on tall stems, typically spring–summer.
Time to maturity
Slow; rosette reaches display size over 1–2 years.
Batch 2

Dendrobium Orchid

Dendrobium nobile hybrid · Orchidaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Himalaya, Southeast Asia, Australia
Care
Bright indirect light with some direct morning sun. After flowering, give a cool dry rest period (10–15°C, reduce watering) in autumn to trigger next year's blooms. Water freely and feed in summer growing season.
Propagation
Keiki (plantlets on canes) once roots are 2–3 cm; division of large clumps.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor; cool rest period in autumn is beneficial.
Sowing
Not grown from seed at home.
Flowering
Masses of purple/white/yellow blooms along canes, typically late winter–spring.
Time to maturity
Bought near-mature; reblooms annually with correct care.
Batch 2

Heartleaf Philodendron

Philodendron hederaceum · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Central America, Caribbean
Care
Bright to moderate indirect light. Water when top 2–3 cm dry. Climbing form benefits from a moss pole. Easy and fast-growing; tolerates lower light.
Propagation
Stem cuttings with a node root readily in water or compost.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~13°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Rare indoors; grown for foliage.
Time to maturity
Fast; fills out within a season.
Calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets if chewed.
Batch 2

Monstera (juvenile)

Monstera deliciosa · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Southern Mexico to Panama
Care
Bright indirect light. Water when top 3–5 cm dry; allow to drain. Provide a support pole as it matures. Wipe leaves; feed in growing season. Juvenile leaves are unlobed — fenestrations develop with maturity.
Propagation
Stem cuttings with a node and aerial root; air-layering.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~13°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Does not flower indoors.
Time to maturity
Moderate–fast; fenestrated adult leaves appear within 2–3 years.
Calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets.
Batch 2

Philodendron 'Dark Lord'

Philodendron 'Dark Lord' · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Colombia (hybrid cultivar)
Care
Bright indirect light enhances dark colouration. Water when top 3–5 cm dry; high humidity preferred. New leaves emerge bright red then darken to near-black.
Propagation
Stem cuttings with a node; air-layering.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~16°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Rare indoors; grown for dramatic foliage.
Time to maturity
Moderate; large leaves develop over a couple of years.
Calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets.
Batch 2

Moth Orchid (dormant/between blooms)

Phalaenopsis hybrid · Orchidaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Southeast Asia & Australia
Care
Same care as your other Phalaenopsis. Currently between blooms — continue watering and feeding. A temperature drop at night (to ~16°C) for 4–6 weeks can trigger a new flower spike.
Propagation
Keiki from nodes; division.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~16°C.
Sowing
Not grown from seed at home.
Flowering
Will rebloom; often winter–spring.
Time to maturity
Reblooms annually.
Same species as Batch 1 entry — catalogued separately as a distinct plant.
Batch 2

Vanilla Orchid

Vanilla planifolia · Orchidaceae
OrnamentalEdibleMedicinal
Origin
Mexico & Central America
Care
Bright indirect light; high humidity (>60%). Keep evenly moist; never dry out completely. Train up a pole or trellis. Warm at all times — above 18°C. Hand-pollinate flowers for vanilla pods.
Propagation
Stem cuttings with 2–3 nodes root in warm humid conditions.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor only, above ~18°C.
Sowing
N/A — propagated by cuttings.
Flowering
Pale yellow-green flowers (currently has/had blooms visible). Each flower open for one day only; hand-pollinate for pods.
Time to maturity
3–5 years to first flowers; pods take 9 months to mature after pollination.
Source of natural vanilla flavouring. One of the few edible orchids.
Batch 2

ZZ Plant

Zamioculcas zamiifolia · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Eastern Africa (Kenya to South Africa)
Care
Extremely tolerant — survives low light and drought. Water sparingly; allow to dry completely between waterings. Stores water in rhizomes. One of the most neglect-tolerant houseplants.
Propagation
Leaf cuttings (slow — 6–9 months); division of rhizomes.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~8°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Rare indoors; insignificant if it occurs.
Time to maturity
Slow; long-lived.
All parts toxic if ingested; wash hands after handling.
Batch 2

Ming Aralia

Polyscias fruticosa · Araliaceae
OrnamentalMedicinal
Origin
India, Pacific Islands (widely cultivated)
Care
Bright indirect light. Keep evenly moist but not waterlogged; hates drought and cold. Dislikes being moved — drops leaves in response to stress. High humidity beneficial. Prune to shape.
Propagation
Stem cuttings with bottom heat; air-layering.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~16°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Insignificant; grown for feathery foliage.
Time to maturity
Moderate–slow; a large specimen takes many years.
Batch 2

Calathea / Prayer Plant (large)

Goeppertia sp. / Calathea sp. · Marantaceae
OrnamentalPet-safe
Origin
South America (tropical)
Care
Indirect light only — direct sun scorches leaves. Keep lightly moist with filtered or rainwater; brown edges indicate dry air or hard water. High humidity, warmth, draught-free.
Propagation
Division at repotting in spring.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~16°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Insignificant indoors.
Time to maturity
Moderate.
Non-toxic / pet-safe. Exact species to be confirmed.
Batch 2

Pothos / Devil's Ivy

Epipremnum aureum · Araceae
OrnamentalAir-purifyingToxic
Origin
Mo'orea, French Polynesia (naturalised tropics worldwide)
Care
Very easy and tolerant. Bright to low indirect light (variegation fades in low light). Water when top few cm dry. Trails or climbs. One of the best air-purifying houseplants.
Propagation
Stem cuttings with a node root readily in water or compost.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~10°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Does not flower indoors.
Time to maturity
Fast — trails metres in a season.
Calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets.
Batch 2

Pilea (trailing)

Pilea nummulariifolia or similar · Urticaceae
OrnamentalPet-safe
Origin
Caribbean, South America
Care
Bright indirect light. Keep evenly moist; does not like to dry out fully. High humidity helps. Trim to encourage bushy growth.
Propagation
Stem cuttings root very easily in water or compost.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~12°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Tiny insignificant flowers.
Time to maturity
Fast-growing trailing plant.
Non-toxic / pet-safe. Exact species to be confirmed.
Batch 2

Tradescantia / Spiderwort

Tradescantia zebrina · Commelinaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Mexico & Central America
Care
Bright indirect light maintains silver/purple variegation — fades in shade. Water when top cm or two dry; tolerates some neglect. Pinch tips to keep bushy.
Propagation
Stem cuttings root in days in water; very easy.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~10°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Small pink/purple flowers sporadically.
Time to maturity
Very fast; fills a pot within months.
Sap can cause skin irritation in some people.
Batch 2

Elephant Ear Alocasia

Alocasia macrorrhiza or A. odora · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Southeast Asia & Pacific Islands
Care
Bright indirect light; large leaves need space. Keep evenly moist; high humidity. Dislikes cold draughts. Can go dormant in winter — reduce watering.
Propagation
Division of offsets/corms; separation of pups.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~15°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Rare indoors.
Time to maturity
Fast-growing once established; large specimen in 2–3 years.
Calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets.
Batch 2

Common Ivy

Hedera helix · Araliaceae
Ornamental
Origin
Europe & Western Asia
Care
Tolerates shade well; bright indirect light for best growth. Keep evenly moist; water less in winter. Hardy outdoors but also a classic trailing houseplant. Watch for spider mites indoors.
Propagation
Stem cuttings root easily in water or compost.
Hardiness
Very hardy outdoors (RHS H5); indoors keep above 5°C.
Sowing
N/A (propagate by cuttings).
Flowering
Flowers on mature adult growth (rare indoors); small greenish clusters.
Time to maturity
Fast-growing; trails extensively.
Berries and leaves mildly toxic if eaten; sap may irritate skin.
Batch 2

Monstera (mature)

Monstera deliciosa · Araceae
OrnamentalToxic
Origin
Southern Mexico to Panama
Care
Bright indirect light. Water when top 3–5 cm dry. Provide a sturdy support. Wipe large leaves; feed monthly in growing season. Same species as juvenile plant — fully fenestrated adult form.
Propagation
Stem cuttings with node and aerial root; air-layering.
Hardiness
Tender — indoor, above ~13°C.
Sowing
N/A
Flowering
Does not flower indoors.
Time to maturity
This is the mature form — fenestrations fully developed.
Calcium oxalate — toxic to people and pets.
Care notes are general guidance for UK indoor/patio growing. ⚠ Cards marked "to be confirmed" need identity verification. Always check toxicity if you have pets or children.
